title: 睇完scmp Columnist篇文，就覺得好撚灰

replies.msg:
https://www.scmp.com/comment/opinion/article/3034637/dont-blame-police-arresting-child-protesters?utm_content=article&utm_medium=Social&utm_source=Facebook&fbclid=IwAR1nXzXLpXgIn1cNAl7HNUQYuaUFr7KcQ9J3BnABKXJd6rXyxOlt5nXHQrM#Echobox=1572038677

Don’t blame police for arresting child protesters
SCMP Columnist
My Take
by Alex Lo

Children need protection, including especially those who have been arrested for taking part in protests or riots. Whether you approve or disapprove of their actions, they are our responsibility.
That’s why police need to streamline established protocol when processing children under detention. The worrying trend is that protesters are getting younger and younger, and more and more children are being arrested. Meanwhile, police manpower has been stretched thin.
Instead of relying on existing procedures, it’s time to involve child welfare specialists from the get-go when someone arrested is underage. That will not only better protect a child’s welfare and rights, but also the force’s reputation.
However, it seems some professional groups in Hong Kong are mired in deep moral confusion when they accuse police of child abuse or worse.
The grandstanding by Hong Kong Paediatric Foundation is a case in point. It’s all very well that the foundation wants to protect the welfare of children. But let’s not throw politics and ideology into the mix.
“We are extremely appalled,” it said in a statement, “to witness violence to our children, horrified and angry with our SAR government for allowing the rights and welfare giving to our children according to the United Nations Convention for the Rights of the Child 1989 being abused and trespassed!
“Physical and any kinds of violence, chemical weapons, plastic bullets, brutal arrest and unfair law enforcement are totally unacceptable because they are detrimental to the health (moral and mental especially) of our children.”
“Physical and any kinds of violence”? By “any”, did it mean verbal “violence” such as harsh language from officers? Chemical weapons? I think it meant tear gas and pepper spray!
If parents allow their own children to “street fight” with police, it’s their responsibility if children are arrested or hurt. When a rioter is covered from head to toe in black, it’s impossible to tell if he or she is 25 or 15. Even you may not be able to distinguish your own child in a rioting crowd.

The foundation is making serious accusations, yet has offered no evidence. The statement, completely unprofessional as it is, was the outcome of a round-table forum attended by 15 self-styled experts. But not a single one of them dared put their names on the statement.
Instead of making unfounded claims and political statements, I suggest those “experts” volunteer to consult police in the handling of children under detention.


一起身睇完篇撚屌文
雖然知條友出名奶共
但都叫知識份子讀過下書
但呢個時間仲寫埋d仆街文去blame the victim
咁多手足連自己自由都斷送埋
想香港呢個家未來更好
但都俾呢d仆街無視
仲要屌你唔識野阻著哂
我自己覺得好撚灰
文妓

左報
